excerpts.query: operation daily use display menu controls operator
excerpts.text:
Select your language, country, 
and time zone, then set up the boat network and follow the on-
screen prompts.
SETUP GUIDE
On the Welcome screen, select Continue device setup to 
complete the device settings. Alternatively, select the settings 
icon 
 , located on the app drawer and the quick access menu, 
then select Setup guide to set up the unit.
 ¼ Note:  Complete the setup to ensure accurate and reliable 
navigation.
QUICK ACCESS MENU
The quick access menu gives you immediate access to basic 
system settings and features. Press the power key or  to 
display the quick access menu. 
 ¼ Note: To hide the quick access menu, tap anywhere on the 
screen.
APPS AND STATUS BAR
An app is a program for a unique feature or function. The 
availability of some apps depends on the size of your unit and 
the hardware that is connected.
For app-specific user guides, download the B&G app on your 
mobile device and connect it to your display unit, or visit:  
www.bandg.com/downloads/zeus-sr.
Select  to open the app drawer, where you can launch apps 
and access system settings. The status bar at the top of the 
screen shows live system status and alerts.

excerpts.query: operation daily use display menu controls operator
excerpts.text:
Zeus
®
 SR
Quick Start Guide
*988-13244-002*
This document describes the basic controls of the B&G Zeus® 
SR multi-function display (MFD).
For the latest version of this document and other app guides, 
download the B&G mobile app, scan the QR code®, and 
connect with your Zeus® SR, or visit:  
www.bandg.com/downloads/zeus-sr. 
 ¼ Note: Refer to the enclosed installation manual before you 
turn on the unit.
BASIC CONTROLS
To turn the unit on, press and hold the power button (G). A 
beep indicates the unit is powering up.
To turn the unit off, press and hold the power button, or select 
Power off from the quick access menu.
A
B
C
D
E
F
G
H
I
A Touchscreen
B Home
C App Menu
D Scroll wheel
E Exit
F Waypoint
G Power
H Customizable keys
• 1x 10" unit
• 2x 12" and 16" units
I Memory card door
POWER CONTROL
Power to the unit can be controlled by an external switch or 
other units. For details about available power options, refer to 
the unit’s installation manual.
 ¼ Note: If your MFD is controlled by an external power source, 
it can’t be powered off using the power button. Instead, pressing 
and holding the power button puts the unit in standby mode. To

excerpts.query: operation daily use display menu controls operator
excerpts.text:
To activate a MOB, select the MOB icon (1) in the quick access 
menu to immediately create a MOB waypoint at your vessel’s 
current location and begin.
Alternatively, open the MOB app (2). This automatically creates 
a MOB waypoint at your vessel’s current location.
After the MOB is activated, select  and follow the on-screen 
instructions to respond to the emergency.
1
2
 ¼ Note: Select Stop Navigation to end MOB navigation. You 
can then Deactivate the MOB.
ALERTS
The unit continuously monitors connected sensors and 
devices for alerts, system faults and environmental changes. 
Alerts display on the top left of your screen and can be 
configured by setting up rules
To view a list of all your alert messages, select  then 
  on 
the quick access menu.
When triggered, an alert rule generates one of the following 
message types:
• Alarm (red)—a message that stays on the screen until you 
mute it. If your system’s siren is on, it is also accompanied 
by an audible alarm. 
• Warning (amber)—a message that stays on the screen until 
you mute it.
• Notification (blue)—an informational message used to 
notify of a non-critical event e.g. software updates.
CONNECT TO MOBILE APP
